channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Teenagers' Online Habits: A Look at Social Media Use and Mental Health Concerns
item.description: American teenagers' online habits, social media use, and mental health concerns in the US
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: GPT is far likelier than other AI models to fabricate quotes by public figures, our analysis shows
item.description: Large language models typically perform so similarly that their differences can be measured by millimeters. But in some scenarios, these models are separated by miles.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Thompson, Fast Company

item.title: Sophia, a famous robot and global icon of AI, wins hearts at Zimbabwe's innovation fair
item.description: Sophia, a world-renowned robot, has been the center of attention at an Artificial Intelligence and Innovation fair in Zimbabwe this week.
item.dc:creator: By Farai Mutsaka, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Google’s Agentspace will put AI agents in the hands of workers
item.description: Google has unveiled an AI agent builder tool designed to automate repititive tasks and help workers find information held across their organization faster. AI agents have become a major focus for software vendors in recent months, including Atlassian, Microsoft, Salesforce, and numerous others.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The race for data and AI expertise in government
item.description: Much is at stake to have a world-class public sector data and AI workforce amid the current global uncertainty and fierce talent competition. The post The race for data and AI expertise in government first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Musk knocks SEC over settlement offer in probe
item.description: Tech mogul Elon Musk criticized the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on Thursday over a settlement offer in the agency’s probe into his 2022 purchase of Twitter, the social platform now known as X.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: US-China Science Agreement Updated Amid Rivalry
item.description: The US updates a decades-old science and tech agreement with China, narrowing its scope and adding safeguards to minimize national security risks.
item.dc:creator: Didi Tang, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New Zealand scientists suspect specimen of world's rarest whale died from head injuries
item.description: Scientists suspect the first complete specimen ever recorded of the world’s rarest whale died from head injuries.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: If you invested in Apple’s IPO 44 years ago, here’s what your shares would be worth today
item.description: On December 12, 1980, a few hours after the opening bell rang on Wall Street, shares of Apple Computer officially began trading. The numbers, by today’s standards, weren’t that impressive.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: Trump backs dockworkers' union preparing to strike over automation
item.description: President-elect Trump backed the dockworkers' union as it prepares for a strike over automation in mid-January — right before the former president is set to return to the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: DoorDash steps up driver ID checks after traffic safety complaints
item.description: DoorDash will require its drivers to verify their identities more often as part of a larger effort to crack down on unauthorized account sharing. DoorDash has been under pressure to ensure its drivers are operating legally.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Sweden gets one of the EU’s seven AI factories
item.description: The new Swedish AI factory is named Mimer and is one of seven now selected by the EU. Mimer is one of five of these AI factories that will now have a brand new AI-optimized supercomputer.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: GSA closes in on enterprisewide software deal with Microsoft
item.description: Laura Stanton, GSA’s assistant commissioner for the IT category, said GSA, OFPP have worked with agencies to come up with 24 standard terms and conditions. The post GSA closes in on enterprisewide software deal with Microsoft first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Character.AI is being sued for encouraging kids to self-harm
item.description: Two families in Texas have filed a new federal product liability lawsuit against Google-backed company Character.AI, accusing it of harming their children. The lawsuit alleges that Character.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: 8 brilliant browser tab tricks for Windows power users
item.description: Web browsers are essential for everything from business productivity to social media and online shopping. Whether you use Google Chrome, Microsoft Edge, Mozilla Firefox, Brave, or something even more exotic, there’s a good chance your web browser is your most used Windows program.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Networking terms and definitions
item.description: To find a brief definition of the networking term you are looking for user your browser’s “Find” feature then follow links to a fuller explanation. AI networking AI networking refers to the application of artificial intelligence (AI) technologies to network management and optimization.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Cato Networks brings IoT, OT support to SASE platform
item.description: Cato Networks today announced new capabilities for its cloud-based service access service edge (SASE) platform that will enable enterprise organizations to more easily manage and secure Internet of Things (IoT) and operational technology (OT) devices.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: California to consider requiring mental health warnings on social media sites
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California, home to some of the largest technology companies in the world, would be the first U.S. state to require mental health warning labels on social media sites if lawmakers pass a bill introduced Monday.
item.dc:creator: TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Don’t scam yourself with the tricks hackers don’t want me to share
item.description: Cybercriminals are tricking people into compromising their own technology by mimicking trusted sources; here's how to keep yourself safe from these "scam-yourself" attacks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
